skill.name: code-review-assistant
skill.description: 自动代码审查 — PR代码→安全扫描+性能分析+最佳实践建议+评审报告
skill.summary: 自动代码审查 — PR代码→安全扫描+性能分析+最佳实践建议+评审报告
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged two high-severity patterns, both confirmed as false positives. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' alert at SKILL.md:4 was triggered by Unicode arrow characters in a Chinese description string, not actual cryptography. The 'High file entropy' alert was caused by UTF-8 encoded Chinese text, which naturally has higher byte entropy than ASCII. The skill contains only a markdown description file with no executable code, no network access, no file system operations, and no command execution. No malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Static False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged line 4 as containing a 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. The actual content is a Chinese description string with Unicode arrow characters. No cryptography is present. This is a false positive caused by non-ASCII symbols matching heuristic patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains only a Chinese marketing description with arrow symbols. No cryptographic operations, imports, or algorithms exist anywhere in the file.

low_findings.title: Static False Positive: High File Entropy
low_findings.description: The static scanner reported 'high file entropy (6.24 bits)' suggesting possible binary or encrypted content. This is a false positive caused by UTF-8 encoded Chinese characters, which use 3 bytes per character and naturally raise byte-level entropy above typical ASCII text. The file is plain human-readable markdown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only plain markdown with Chinese text. UTF-8 Chinese characters naturally have higher entropy than ASCII. Content is fully human-readable with no binary or encrypted data.

content.user_title: Review Pull Requests for Security and Quality
content.value_statement: Code reviews are time-consuming and often miss security vulnerabilities or performance issues. This skill automatically analyzes pull requests to catch bugs, security risks, and style violations before they reach production.
actual_capabilities: Analyze code for security vulnerabilities and unsafe patterns
Detect performance bottlenecks and optimization opportunities
Check compliance with language-specific best practices
Generate structured review reports with actionable recommendations
Support multiple programming languages and frameworks
limitations: Cannot execute or test code; analysis is static only
May produce false positives that require human verification
Requires access to source code to perform analysis
Does not replace senior engineer judgment on architecture decisions

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Security Scan
use_cases.description: Scan pull requests for security vulnerabilities before merging to main branch

use_cases.title: Code Quality Review
use_cases.description: Review code style, naming conventions, and anti-patterns in team submissions

use_cases.title: Learning Tool for Juniors
use_cases.description: Learn best practices by reviewing automated feedback on personal projects

prompt_templates.title: Basic PR Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request for bugs, security issues, and code style problems
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick review of a small change

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a deep security audit of this code. Look for injection risks, unsafe deserialization, hardcoded secrets, and authentication flaws
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewing code that handles user data

prompt_templates.title: Performance Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this code for performance bottlenecks. Check for N+1 queries, memory leaks, inefficient algorithms, and blocking operations

prompt_templates.title: Full Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PR comprehensively: security, performance, maintainability, test coverage, and architectural fit. Provide a prioritized action plan

output_examples.input: Review this Python function that handles user login
output_examples.output: SECURITY: Line 12 uses MD5 for password hashing — switch to bcrypt or Argon2. PERFORMANCE: Line 8 queries the database in a loop — use a JOIN instead. STYLE: Function name 'doThing' is unclear — rename to 'authenticate_user'.

output_examples.input: Check this React component for issues
output_examples.output: BUG: useEffect on line 15 has a missing dependency 'userId', causing stale closures. ACCESSIBILITY: Button on line 22 lacks an aria-label. PERFORMANCE: Large list on line 30 should use virtualization.

SECURITY: No input validation on 'email' parameter — add regex or schema validation. SECURITY: SQL query on line 45 uses string concatenation — use parameterized queries. PERFORMANCE: No rate limiting on this endpoint.

best_practices: Provide the full file context, not just the diff, for more accurate analysis
Review the skill output manually before acting on critical security findings
Use specific prompts for focused reviews rather than broad requests
anti_patterns: Blindly applying every suggestion without understanding the context
Using the skill as a substitute for peer review in a team setting
Ignoring false positives instead of refining the prompt for clarity

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill works with any programming language including Python, JavaScript, Java, Go, Rust, and more.

faq.question: Does this skill execute my code?
faq.answer: No. The analysis is entirely static and does not run or compile your code.

faq.question: Can this skill replace human code reviewers?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to assist reviewers, not replace them. Always verify critical findings.

faq.question: How accurate are the security findings?
faq.answer: The skill uses pattern matching and heuristics. Some findings may be false positives.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What types of issues can it detect?
faq.answer: Security vulnerabilities, performance problems, style violations, and logical bugs.
